channel.title: maGaming RSS Feed - www.gamedeveloper.com
channel.description: A cleaned-up version of the original gaming feed for www.gamedeveloper.com

item.title: Sony is laying off 'most' of the Destiny team and 'some' Marathon devs
item.content:encoded:
Image via Bungie/PlayStation

Sony Interactive Entertainment (SIE), Bungie's parent company, is laying off "most" of the Destiny team, "some" Marathon developers, and doing a number of reductions across SIE teams that support the developer's operations.

SIE CEO Hermen Hulst shared the news in a blog post this morning, saying that those impacted at Bungie and within SIE are "being informed today" as well. While Hulst didn't share a specific figure, he says the workforce reductions are affecting a "significant number" of employees.

"This decision was made only after extensive discussion and careful consideration, and I want to provide some context on how we arrived here," the CEO says. "Over the past several months, together with Bungie leadership, we reviewed the studio's long-term direction, development priorities, resource needs, and role within our broader portfolio strategy. We explored multiple alternatives before concluding that a reduction was necessary to align the studio's resources with its current priorities and long-term goals."

Related:Italian studio 34BigThings regains independence from Embracer

Last month, Bungie announced it was ending live development on Destiny 2, with a June 9 update serving as a farewell. The news came after Sony's reveal last fall that Destiny 2 was not performing to the parent company's expectations.

As a reminder, Sony famously purchased Bungie for $3.6 billion in 2022, around five years after Destiny 2's original release.

Four years later, following the news about the end of live development, Bloomberg reported that Sony was preparing to jettison a "significant" number of Bungie workers.

Bloomberg's report also mentioned that Bungie is now pivoting its efforts to Marathon, which Hulst has confirmed in today's announcement.

"Marathon remains an important part of our portfolio, and we will continue to support the team as they build on the strong foundation established in Season 1 and 2, and as they work on incubation efforts for future projects. While it's too early to discuss, we are encouraged by the creativity and opportunities that lie ahead."

Hulst adds that SIE's immediate priority is for its affected employees, providing "transition support" and, "where possible," working to identify opportunities across SIE and the company's global network of studios.

"I know today's news is deeply difficult not only for those leaving, but for those colleagues and friends that remain. Please take the time you need to process this news and support one another," Hulst said.

Game Developer has contacted Sony Interactive Entertainment for more information on the matter.

Related:Magic: The Gathering Arena developers have successfully unionized

About the Author

Contributing Editor, News, GameDeveloper.com

Diego Nicolás Argüello is a freelance journalist and critic from Argentina. Video games helped him to learn English, so now he covers them for places like The New York Times, NPR, Rolling Stone, and more. He also runs Into the Spine, a site dedicated to fostering and supporting new writers, and co-hosted Turnabout Breakdown, a podcast about the Ace Attorney series. He’s most likely playing a rhythm game as you read this.



https://www.gamedeveloper.com/business/sony-is-laying-off-most-of-the-destiny-team-and-some-marathon-devs
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot
